std-agent-completion.name: std-agent-completion
std-agent-completion.description: Wraps llm/generate — takes a prompt, returns generated text.

std-agent-context.name: std-agent-context
std-agent-context.description: Wraps llm/token-count + llm/compact — context window management.

std-agent-memory.name: std-agent-memory
std-agent-memory.description: Wraps memory/recall + memory/store — semantic memory for agent loops.

std-agent-session.name: std-agent-session
std-agent-session.description: Wraps session/read-spec + session/write-spec + session/read-history — orbital session data.

std-agent-tool-call.name: std-agent-tool-call
std-agent-tool-call.description: Wraps llm/call-tools — takes messages + tools, returns tool-call result.

std-agent-trace.name: std-agent-trace
std-agent-trace.description: Wraps trace/emit + trace/log — observability for agent loops.

std-agent-fix-loop.name: std-agent-fix-loop
std-agent-fix-loop.description: Agent fix-loop molecule — validate an orbital, generate a repair prompt if errors, apply fix.

std-agent-planner.name: std-agent-planner
std-agent-planner.description: Agent planner molecule — recall memories, then generate a plan from the prompt + recalled context.

std-agent-tool-loop.name: std-agent-tool-loop
std-agent-tool-loop.description: Agent tool-loop molecule — call-tools, check response, emit result for iteration.

std-agent-builder.name: std-agent-builder
std-agent-builder.description: Agent builder organism — plan, build, validate, fix a single orbital end-to-end.

std-agent-rabit.name: std-agent-rabit
std-agent-rabit.description: Rabit organism — coordinator decomposes a prompt into orbital specs, dispatches subagent builds, monitors completion, assembles and validates the composed schema.

std-anim-tick.name: std-anim-tick
std-anim-tick.description: std-anim-tick — sprite/effects animation clock atom. Advances every unit's frame (mod frameCount) and ages @entity.effects (decrement ttl, drop expired) every tick. Boards compose it via `uses` + a sibling trait-ref rebound (linkedEntity) to a board entity that carries `units` + `effects`, replacing the inlined animTick that was duplicated across 8+ game boards.

std-approval-gate.name: std-approval-gate
std-approval-gate.description: std-approval-gate — generic approval gate / approver workflow. Use when a user voices that an action must be reviewed, approved, signed off, or authorized by a privileged role before it proceeds (admin approval, supervisor sign-off, manager approval, gatekeeper review, editor approval, senior editor approval, reviewer approval, publishing approval, draft approval, content review, submit drafts then editors review and approve or reject, needs approval before going live, requires sign-off before publish). Persists ApprovalRequest rows + surfaces them in a review queue with Approve / Deny / Reject actions, optionally requiring a written rejection reason or feedback comment. On approval emits ApprovalGranted; on denial emits ApprovalDenied. Compose onto any orbital whose action you want to gate: the target orbital emits an external event, an ApprovalRequest row is created, and downstream effects wait on ApprovalGranted. Differs from std-mod-queue, which is content-moderation-specific.

std-audit-capture.name: std-audit-capture
std-audit-capture.description: std-audit-capture — event-interceptor audit log / audit trail. Use when a user voices that a record's changes must be tracked, logged, or auditable (audit trail, track changes, change history, who edited what, mutation log, record changes, log every edit, edit history, modification log, who did what when, accountability log). Listens for the generic `EntityMutated` sentinel (rename via traitOverrides.events to the real mutation event, e.g. ContactSaved) and persists an AuditEntry row capturing actor, action, entity type/id, before/after snapshots, and timestamp. `captureEventTypes` filters on @payload.data.kind (empty = capture all). Compose with `std-lifecycle` to apply retention. Composable via `extraTraits` to interpose audit logging behind any mutating trait.

std-cache-aside.name: std-cache-aside
std-cache-aside.description: infra atom — std-cache-aside (states: cached, empty, error, loading, stale)

std-cascade-on-delete.name: std-cascade-on-delete
std-cascade-on-delete.description: std-cascade-on-delete — relational `owns` cascade. Listens for a parent-deleted sentinel (`ParentDeleted` — rename via traitOverrides.events to the consumer's actual parent-deleted event), then deletes every dependent row whose `foreignKeyField` points at the deleted parent — via a self-driving loop (delete the head of the matching set, re-fetch until none remain). Emits `CascadeRowDeleted` per row and `CascadeCompleted` when done.

std-circuit-breaker.name: std-circuit-breaker
std-circuit-breaker.description: infra atom — std-circuit-breaker (states: closed, halfOpen, open)

std-cooldown.name: std-cooldown
std-cooldown.description: std-cooldown — ability/action cooldown primitive; START begins the countdown, READY fires when it expires.

std-cross-reference.name: std-cross-reference
std-cross-reference.description: std-cross-reference — join-table primitive for N:M relationships. Listens for LinkRequested/UnlinkRequested/FETCH_LINKS_FOR and manages the junction collection (default CrossRefLink [persistent: cross_ref_links]). Rebind via linkedEntity to your consumer junction. Emits LinkCreated, LinkRemoved, LinksLoaded as external broadcasts for downstream consumers.

std-data-erasure.name: std-data-erasure
std-data-erasure.description: std-data-erasure — GDPR Art. 17 right-to-be-forgotten queue. Tracks ErasureRequest rows through pending → grace → executed lifecycle with Cancel during grace. v1.0 ships the request lifecycle UI + knob set; auto-emit of ExecuteErasure after grace lands in v1.1 (loop-with-emit gap — docs/Almadar_Std_Factories.md). Target app/ atoms then own the actual delete or piiFields scrub via an ExecuteErasure listener.

std-game-clock.name: std-game-clock
std-game-clock.description: std-game-clock — fixed-timestep authoritative clock games compose to drive time; emits GameClockTicked every 100ms while running.

std-lifecycle.name: std-lifecycle
std-lifecycle.description: std-lifecycle — age-based status lifecycle for a target entity. A periodic tick scans the target for records in `fromStatus` whose `dateField` is older than `ageDays`, and transitions each one to `toStatus` — every matching record, via a self-driving scan loop (process the head of a filtered fetch, persist the status, re-fetch until the set drains). Compose onto any orbital and bind `targetEntity` + the status/date knobs.

std-mod-queue.name: std-mod-queue
std-mod-queue.description: std-mod-queue — moderation queue for content review. Owns a ModQueueItem entity (one row per flagged target) and a single review trait that loads pending items, renders them in a data-grid with Approve / Reject / Escalate actions, and persists the reviewer's verdict back to the row.

std-notify-on-event.name: std-notify-on-event
std-notify-on-event.description: std-notify-on-event — cross-orbital wiring atom. Subscribes to events from another orbital (e.g. CheckoutOrbital emits OrderPlaced, OrderRecordOrbital reacts via this atom) and dispatches a notification. Use when one orbital needs to react to or be notified about events that happen in another orbital — the wiring goes through this atom's `EventOccurred` listener which the molecule renames via traitOverrides.events to the upstream event name. Resolves recipient from `recipients` (first entry; multi-recipient fan-out is a future extension) or falls back to `@user.id`. Skips dispatch when the recipient is in `suppressionList`. Emits in-app `notify` when `notifyChannel` is `"in-app"`, or persists a `NotificationRecord` row for out-of-band delivery when channel is `"email"` or `"sms"`.

std-reminder-scheduler.name: std-reminder-scheduler
std-reminder-scheduler.description: std-reminder-scheduler — deadline reminders for a target entity. A periodic tick scans the target for records whose `dateField` deadline is within `offsetHours` of now and that have not been reminded yet, and emits `ReminderDue` for each — every matching record, via a self-driving scan loop. Marks each record `remindedAt` so it isn't reminded twice. Compose alongside std-notify-on-event to deliver the reminder.

std-row-access-control.name: std-row-access-control
std-row-access-control.description: std-row-access-control — row-level access control gate / ownership filter / role-based row visibility. Use when a user voices that only certain users should see or edit certain records (only the owner can edit, private to owner, owner-only visibility, only see your own records, role-based visibility, admin-only access, visible to admins only, restricted to role, permission gate, ownership scoping, hide from other users, only show mine). Listens for `RowsLoaded` (rename via traitOverrides.events to the consumer's load event), filters `@payload.data` by `@user.id` (when `ownerField` is set) and/or `@user.role` (when `visibleRoles` is non-empty), re-emits `RowsFiltered` for downstream consumers (rename to wire into the UI atom's listen). Composable via `extraTraits` to interpose between a data fetch and a list/grid UI.

std-status-lifecycle.name: std-status-lifecycle
std-status-lifecycle.description: Infra substrate: data-driven entity status lifecycle. The statuses and the allowed transitions are config data over a fixed single-state topology, so a host organism can expand or collapse its lifecycle without forking. Compose onto an entity, rename the ChangeStatusRequested listen to the host's status-change action, and supply `states` + `transitions`. A requested move is validated against the `transitions` table (illegal moves are rejected), then the new value is written to `statusField` on the target entity and StatusChanged is emitted. Surfaces nothing — the host renders the badge and buttons.

ui-behavior-view.name: ui-behavior-view
ui-behavior-view.description: BehaviorView — UI factory (1:1 with the @almadar/ui behavior-view pattern).

ui-module-card.name: ui-module-card
ui-module-card.description: ModuleCard — UI factory (1:1 with the @almadar/ui module-card pattern).

std-app-layout.name: std-app-layout
std-app-layout.description: std-app-layout — dashboard-layout chrome (sidebar nav + top bar with search + topBarActions + notifications) wrapping a single contentTrait slot. Use for list-style apps (CMS / helpdesk / CRM): the chrome stays stable while the consumer's content trait renders the data, and the consumer can add domain icons (cart / profile / help) via topBarActions. Bind once per orbital — the consumer rebinds linkedEntity via `XAppLayout = AppShell.traits.AppLayout -> XEntity` so multiple orbitals in one file each get their own chrome without colliding on a shared atom entity.

std-app-search.name: std-app-search
std-app-search.description: std-app-search — reusable global search: wraps a host content view and, on a SEARCH trigger, fetches the linked entity filtered by @config.searchField and renders the matching results, then CLEAR_SEARCH returns to the host view. Rebind to any entity; point @config.idleContent at the normal view and wire a trigger (e.g. AppLayout SEARCH) into SEARCH.

std-billable-hour.name: std-billable-hour
std-billable-hour.description: std-billable-hour — generic billable-hour timesheet primitive for legal, agency, and consulting domains. Lists entries in `browsing`; opens entry detail in `viewing_single` with Invoice / Write Off actions.

std-board.name: std-board
std-board.description: std-board — generic N-column board primitive. `config.columns` is an array of column definitions ({ key, label, icon, variant }); the trait `array/map`s them on `BoardItemsLoaded` into `@entity.boards` where each board carries its filtered items + a count. The render is a `data-grid` over `@entity.boards` (one card per column) whose `renderItem` lambda nests a `data-list` over `@col.items` (the cards in that stage). N is config-driven — pass any number of columns; the outer `data-grid`'s `cols` is bound to `@config.gridCols` so the grid wraps if you exceed it. Topology = loading / viewing_board / viewing_card / adding / error — distinct from the cookie-cutter `loading/browsing/viewing_single/error` CRUD shape. App-level atoms (e.g. std-pipeline) compose std-board via `uses` and add a sibling persistor + extended source entity. `config.boardLook` switches the board body between the default N-column grid ('columns') and a Trello-style wide-column kanban ('kanban-classic').

std-browse.name: std-browse
std-browse.description: std-browse as a Function — single primitive: data-grid. Owns the fetch + REFETCH (query/filter/page) state machine. Consumers configure fields/imageField/itemActions/cols/gap/variant; the atom renders the grid. Each `fields[]` entry accepts `{ name, label, variant }` plus optional `referenceEntity` + `displayField` to render a foreign-key column as the joined entity's display value (data-grid resolves @list[referenceEntity] at render time when both keys are present). `browseLook` (table default / dense / feed / gallery) selects one of four render bodies; dense/feed/gallery were folded in from std-browse-dense/-feed/-gallery (Phase 5.B) and their consumers retarget by setting browseLook.

std-calendar.name: std-calendar
std-calendar.description: std-calendar as a Function — owns the fetch + SELECT_DAY state machine; default bodyContent renders the month-grid + upcoming-events tree. `config.calendarLook` switches the body between the default month-grid ('month'), a day-grouped agenda list ('agenda-list'), and a 7-column week timeline ('week-timeline') while inheriting the same trait, state machine, emits, and listens.

std-confirmation.name: std-confirmation
std-confirmation.description: Modal-only confirmation trait: renders ONLY to the `modal` slot. Consumers configure icon/title/alertMessage/confirmLabel at the trait-ref call site via `config: { ... }`. Side effects (persist delete, notify, etc.) belong in a sibling coordinator trait that `listens { ConfirmTrait CONFIRM -> DO_DELETE }`.

std-dashboard-grid-embedded.name: std-dashboard-grid-embedded
std-dashboard-grid-embedded.description: std-dashboard-grid-embedded — embeddable sibling of std-dashboard-grid-layout. Renders the same toolbar / filter / 12-tile grid contract WITHOUT wrapping it in dashboard-layout chrome (no sidebar, no appName, no nav). Use inside another app shell that already owns the surrounding chrome.

std-date-range.name: std-date-range
std-date-range.description: std-date-range — compact calendar button (Looker / Tableau shape) that opens a popover containing the date-range-picker. Owns a DateRange runtime entity; DATE_RANGE_CHANGED persists @payload.from + @payload.to so cross-trait listeners can route the range into data atoms (Browse REFETCH_FILTER, Stats / Graphs requery).

std-esign-flow.name: std-esign-flow
std-esign-flow.description: std-esign-flow — the send-for-signature flow: list signature requests, create one (pick document + recipient), resend or cancel, track status. Composes std-file-store + std-esign-request; no document-rendering or code-preview chrome.

std-esign-request.name: std-esign-request
std-esign-request.description: std-esign-request — specialized e-signature request tracker: send a document out for signing and track its status (draft/sent/viewed/signed/declined/expired). Just the request list + send/revoke; signature capture is std-signature-capture's job.

std-event-log.name: std-event-log
std-event-log.description: std-event-log — generic chronological event feed (Stripe activity log / GitHub PR timeline / Linear event feed style). Renders a vertical `timeline` with type-coded dots, a single horizontal chip-bar of kind filters (All + per-kind), reverse-chronological sort, and an inline form for logging events that weren't auto-captured. `config.kindOptions` maps each kind → status/icon/label so consuming molecules/organisms get type-coded visuals without re-implementing topology. Topology = loading / viewing / backfilling / error.

std-export.name: std-export
std-export.description: std-export — a dropdown format menu (CSV/XLSX/PDF defaults; caller can override via config.formats). Emits EXPORT_REQUESTED { itemId, label } on the bus — itemId is the picked format id; host or sibling orbital generates the file. tileId is a static per-instance config value, not echoed in the click payload.

std-file-store.name: std-file-store
std-file-store.description: std-file-store — the generic file/page tree primitive. A file-tree + config-driven data table over a hierarchical collection; opens a row in `viewing_single` with config-driven detail actions. Rebindable: pass `fields`/`itemActions`/`detailActions`/`tree` to retarget it at any hierarchical entity (files, wiki pages, note trees). Folds in the former std-page-tree via `reorderable` + a page field/action set.

std-filter.name: std-filter
std-filter.description: std-filter — single primitive: filter-group. Owns a FilterTarget runtime entity; every FILTER transition persists field/value so cross-trait listeners can route the latest selection into data atoms.

std-form-advanced.name: std-form-advanced
std-form-advanced.description: std-form-advanced — full-typed form-section primitive. Hosts the `editing` state with a configurable `fields` array (each entry mirrors @almadar/ui's `form-section` field schema: `{ name, label, type?, values?, relation?, required? }`). Field-level constraints live in `config.fieldValidation` as `[{ field, format?: 'email'|'url'|'phone', regex?, minLength?, maxLength?, message? }]` — form-section surfaces inline errors when a submit violates a declared constraint. Agents drive shape by overriding `config.fields` + `config.fieldValidation` per-call-site.

std-gallery.name: std-gallery
std-gallery.description: std-gallery — image gallery primitive. Browses GalleryItems in a media-gallery grid; opens a lightbox modal for full-size viewing.

std-geosearch.name: std-geosearch
std-geosearch.description: std-geosearch — location-aware search variant: search-input + map-view + range-slider for radius. Owns a GeoSearchResult runtime entity; GEO_SEARCH persists @payload.searchTerm/lat/lng/radiusKm so cross-trait listeners can route them into geo-aware data atoms.

std-graphs.name: std-graphs
std-graphs.description: std-graphs — embeddable chart atom. ITEMS_LOADED groups @payload.data by @config.categoryField (or @config.timeField when timeAxis=true) and aggregates per bucket. Multi-series rendering via @config.series (preformatted ChartSeries shape). Histograms via chartType=histogram pass pre-binned @payload.data through unchanged. Stacked / 100% stacked bars via @config.stack. Post-process sort + limit. Clicking a data point emits UI:@config.drillEvent with { label, value, seriesLabel? }. Drop into a dashboard alongside std-stats / std-browse.

std-image-upload-multi.name: std-image-upload-multi
std-image-upload-multi.description: std-image-upload-multi — multi-image upload with drop zone, gallery preview, and per-batch progress.

std-list.name: std-list
std-list.description: Daisy-chain composition showcase: no `on EVENT { ... }` trait-ref overrides. Modal + Confirmation trait refs are configured via `config: { ... }` at the call site. Side effects (persist) live in a sibling coordinator trait (`ListItemPersistor`) that listens for the modal/confirmation trait emits. Mirrors std-cart's structure. std-list omits the read-only VIEW path that the old version had — form-section's mode set only supports "create" | "edit", and a dedicated detail-view pattern is tracked separately (std-detail).

std-modal.name: std-modal
std-modal.description: Modal-only trait: renders ONLY to the `modal` slot. The parent molecule's layout/browse trait is responsible for the `main` slot (the list, the trigger buttons, etc.). Consumers configure icon/title/fields/mode at the trait-ref call site via `config: { ... }`. Side effects (persist, notify) belong in a sibling coordinator trait that `listens { ModalTrait SAVE -> DO_PERSIST }`.

std-multi-party-flow.name: std-multi-party-flow
std-multi-party-flow.description: std-multi-party-flow — Escrow.com / DocuSign / Stripe Connect style multi-party transaction. Header + summary (subject/amount/reference) + named parties (avatar/role/actor) + stage progress + your-turn panel + activity feed + dispute/cancel escape hatches. Topology = loading / awaiting / capturing_dispute / capturing_cancel / funded / released / disputed / cancelled / error.
std-multi-party-flow.statePattern: awaiting, cancelled, capturing_cancel, capturing_dispute, disputed, error, funded, loading, released

std-pagination.name: std-pagination
std-pagination.description: std-pagination — single primitive: pagination control. PAGE accepts the Pagination component's `{page}` emit; ITEMS_LOADED accepts an upstream `{data:[ObjectSpec]}` payload and derives totalPages = ceil(len/pageSize). Molecules wire `BrowseItemLoaded -> ITEMS_LOADED` to keep it in lockstep with the data hub.

std-rating-review.name: std-rating-review
std-rating-review.description: std-rating-review — Airbnb / Amazon / Google Maps style review surface. Header with aggregate score + Write CTA, per-star distribution bars, working sort chips (recent / top rated / lowest), scrollable reviews list with author + stars + comment + helpful action. Topology = loading / viewing / composing / submitting / submitted / error.
std-rating-review.statePattern: composing, error, loading, submitted, submitting, viewing

std-recurrence.name: std-recurrence
std-recurrence.description: std-recurrence — recurring-rule definition + upcoming runs (Stripe Subscriptions / Cron Hub style). The viewing state shows a plain-English rule sentence and the next N dated runs as an agenda timeline. Per-run exception modal supports skip / reschedule. Topology = loading / defining_rule / viewing / awaiting_exception_decision / cancelled / error.
std-recurrence.statePattern: awaiting_exception_decision, cancelled, defining_rule, error, loading, viewing

std-related.name: std-related
std-related.description: Relation-rendering atom. Assumes the linked entity's relation field has already been hydrated by an upstream fetch with include; this atom only owns the rendering of the related children. Paired with std-browse via @trait.X this gives a one-line master-detail layout: hstack [ @trait.ParentList, @trait.ParentRelated ]. The molecule std-related-master-detail is the canonical showcase.

std-rich-editor.name: std-rich-editor
std-rich-editor.description: std-rich-editor — wiki/CMS Notion-style block editor. Browses documents in `browsing`; opens the rich-block-editor on a selected row; persists block edits + publish status.

std-saved-search.name: std-saved-search
std-saved-search.description: std-saved-search — persisted query + alert subscription. Owns a SavedSearch entity (one row per saved query) and a single manage trait that lists saved searches in a data-grid, lets users toggle their alert subscription / rename the search / delete it, and persists each change.

std-scatter.name: std-scatter
std-scatter.description: std-scatter — embeddable scatter / bubble chart atom. ITEMS_LOADED maps @payload.data into {x,y,label,size,color} points using configured fields (no aggregation step — distinct from std-graphs). Drop into a dashboard alongside std-stats / std-graphs for two-numeric correlation views.

std-search.name: std-search
std-search.description: std-search — single primitive: search-input. Owns a SearchResult runtime entity; SEARCH persists @payload.searchTerm (matching SearchInput's emit shape) so cross-trait listeners can route it into data atoms (Browse REFETCH_QUERY).

std-selection.name: std-selection
std-selection.description: ui/core atom — std-selection (states: error, idle, loading, selected, selecting)

std-signature-capture.name: std-signature-capture
std-signature-capture.description: std-signature-capture — a drawn-signature pad. The signer draws, clears, or submits; on submit the signature persists and the atom emits SignatureSubmitted. One job: capture a signature and report when done.

std-stats.name: std-stats
std-stats.description: std-stats — embeddable analytics-card atom. ITEMS_LOADED aggregates @payload.data per @config.metrics (count|sum|avg|min|max|variance with optional filter, baselineFilter, format, icon, max, target, trend, trendPolarity, sparkline) and renders one stat-display per entry. Variance metrics compute actual - baseline using `filter` + `baselineFilter` lambdas and report the signed delta as trend. clickEvent emits UI:{clickEvent} { metricLabel } when a card is clicked. Drop into a dashboard alongside std-browse / std-search / std-filter. `config.statsLook` switches the whole body between the default per-metric card grid ('cards'), a Stripe-dashboard KPI tile row ('kpi-tiles'), a single hero metric card ('hero-metric'), and a chart-first sparkline tile grid ('sparkline-row').

std-step-flow.name: std-step-flow
std-step-flow.description: std-step-flow — generic N-step sequential decision chain (Stripe Connect / TurboTax / Vercel deploy style). `config.steps` is an array of step definitions `{ key, label, description, icon, allowedRoles?: [string] }`; the trait projects the current step into a prominent body card and a `wizard-progress` stepper bar at the top. When a step declares `allowedRoles`, only users whose `@user.role` is in that list can advance through it. One primary button ADVANCEs along the chain; at the last step the same button finalizes (label/icon/variant swap via entity binding, state routed via guard). REJECT/ESCALATE terminate. Topology = loading / running / approved / rejected / escalated / error.
std-step-flow.statePattern: approved, error, escalated, loading, rejected, running

std-tabs.name: std-tabs
std-tabs.description: std-tabs — embeddable tab-navigation atom. Renders a tabs strip from @config.tabs ([{id, label, icon?, badge?, content?}]); clicking a tab updates @entity.activeTab so only the matching @trait.X content shows. Events remap at the call site for routing, persistence, analytics.

std-tag-taxonomy.name: std-tag-taxonomy
std-tag-taxonomy.description: std-tag-taxonomy — hierarchical category tag tree (parent/child). TagBrowse fetches Tag rows on INIT, renders the active level via data-grid, and on SELECT_TAG drills into a tag's children by binding @entity.id to the chosen parent.

std-thread.name: std-thread
std-thread.description: std-thread — forum-style nested replies with collapse + flat-mode toggle. Owns ThreadPost (persistent). flat:true covers 1:1 linear inquiry threads; flat:false renders forum-style nested. INIT fetches; REPLY persists a child post under @payload.parentId. Layer 3 variants override `bodyContent` to deliver chat-bubble, email-threaded, or linear-comments UX while inheriting the same trait, state machine, emits, and listens.

std-version-history.name: std-version-history
std-version-history.description: std-version-history — wiki/CMS revision history with diff + rollback. Browses revisions; opens detail in `viewing_single`; compares two revisions in `comparing` with intrinsic version-diff; persists rollback.

std-vote.name: std-vote
std-vote.description: std-vote — forum-style up/down vote tally with single-vote-per-user constraint. Owns a Vote entity; VOTE persists the new direction (toggling to none when re-clicking the active arrow) and emits VoteCasted so cross-trait listeners can recompute aggregates.

std-wizard.name: std-wizard
std-wizard.description: std-wizard — generic N-step data-collection wizard (Vercel deploy / GitHub repo create / Stripe Connect onboarding style). Each step has its own form fields. Topology = loading / running / completed / cancelled / error. ADVANCE is guard-routed: not-last-step advances; last-step transitions to completed. Distinct from std-step-flow (approval-decision) — wizard collects data via form-section per step, step-flow collects yes/no decisions.

ui-aside.name: ui-aside
ui-aside.description: Aside — UI factory (1:1 with the @almadar/ui aside pattern).

ui-atlas-image.name: ui-atlas-image
ui-atlas-image.description: AtlasImage — UI factory (1:1 with the @almadar/ui atlas-image pattern).

ui-atlas-panel.name: ui-atlas-panel
ui-atlas-panel.description: AtlasPanel — UI factory (1:1 with the @almadar/ui atlas-panel pattern).

ui-avatar.name: ui-avatar
ui-avatar.description: Avatar — UI factory (1:1 with the @almadar/ui avatar pattern).

ui-badge.name: ui-badge
ui-badge.description: Badge — UI factory (1:1 with the @almadar/ui badge pattern).

ui-box.name: ui-box
ui-box.description: Box — UI factory (1:1 with the @almadar/ui box pattern).

ui-button.name: ui-button
ui-button.description: Button — UI factory (1:1 with the @almadar/ui button pattern).

ui-card.name: ui-card
ui-card.description: Card — UI factory (1:1 with the @almadar/ui card pattern).

ui-center.name: ui-center
ui-center.description: Center — UI factory (1:1 with the @almadar/ui center pattern).

ui-checkbox.name: ui-checkbox
ui-checkbox.description: Checkbox — UI factory (1:1 with the @almadar/ui checkbox pattern).

ui-conditional-wrapper.name: ui-conditional-wrapper

std-arcade-flow.name: std-arcade-flow
std-arcade-flow.description: Headless win/lose authority for the arcade play-for-tickets loop: owns only result. Listens SCORE_CHANGED (tickets + tokens) and sets result to won once tickets reach the target, lost when tokens run out first, else none. Renders nothing.

std-arcade-play.name: std-arcade-play
std-arcade-play.description: Headless arcade play-economy mechanic: the SOLE writer of tokens + tickets + machines. Applies a PLAY_MACHINE intent (spends a token, rolls a pseudo-random ticket payout within a configured range, emits BURST + SCORE_CHANGED). Renders nothing.

std-chase-ai.name: std-chase-ai
std-chase-ai.description: Headless chase-AI mechanic: enemies damage adjacent targets, else step toward the nearest target respecting tile passability and occupancy.

std-dungeon-combat.name: std-dungeon-combat
std-dungeon-combat.description: Headless dungeon combat authority: owns the enemy roster, hero HP, and kill score. Bump-attacks damage enemies; monster ticks chase the hero and deal contact damage.

std-fx-particles.name: std-fx-particles
std-fx-particles.description: Headless floating-text/particle mechanic: decays each fx entry's ttl every tick and drops the dead ones, and appends spawned particles on BURST. Composed onto a [runtime, shared] game entity; renders nothing (the board owns the single render authority).

std-gather.name: std-gather
std-gather.description: Headless resource-gathering mechanic: owns features + wood + stone. Listens HERO_STEPPED and, when the hero lands on a wood/stone-type feature, increments the matching resource, removes the node, emits a BURST pickup particle, and emits RESOURCES so survival-flow can decide loss. Never writes units.

std-gold.name: std-gold
std-gold.description: Headless gold-economy mechanic: the SOLE writer of gold. Listens a mine-hit signal and adds a fixed yield; emits GOLD_CHANGED. Renders nothing.

std-grid-tactics.name: std-grid-tactics
std-grid-tactics.description: Headless grid-tactics unit-authority: the SOLE writer of units. Applies MOVE/ATTACK/SPAWN/HEAL intents from any source (player, AI, production, or progression) and runs the enemy AI_TURN pipeline, emitting BURST hit-particles and UNITS_CHANGED survivor counts. Renders nothing.

std-hero-nav.name: std-hero-nav
std-hero-nav.description: Headless single-hero navigation authority: the SOLE writer of units + movesLeft on objective boards. Applies a budget-limited MOVE intent (steps the hero one tile, spends a move, emits HERO_STEPPED) and an optional WANDER intent (random step for neutral NPCs). Renders nothing.

std-market-flow.name: std-market-flow
std-market-flow.description: Headless win/lose authority for market-service boards: owns only result. Listens SERVICE_CHANGED (served + lost) and sets result to won when enough customers are served, lost when too many patience timers expire, else none. Renders nothing.

std-objective-flow.name: std-objective-flow
std-objective-flow.description: Headless win/lose authority for objective boards: owns only result. Listens PROGRESS (objectivesLeft + movesLeft) and sets result to won when every objective is reached, lost when the move budget runs out first, else none. Renders nothing.

std-objective-reach.name: std-objective-reach
std-objective-reach.description: Headless reach-the-objective mechanic: owns features + score + objectivesLeft. Listens HERO_STEPPED and, when the hero lands on a target-type feature, scores it (optionally removing the model), emits a BURST pickup particle, and emits PROGRESS so round-flow can decide win/lose. Never writes units.

std-patience.name: std-patience
std-patience.description: Headless customer-service mechanic: owns a stationary customer roster with per-customer patience. Decrements patience every tick, serves nearby customers on HERO_STEPPED (resets their patience + scores), and emits CUSTOMER_LOST when a timer expires. Never writes units.

std-production.name: std-production
std-production.description: Headless unit-production mechanic: accrues productionPoints each turn (AI_TURN) and, on an affordable PRODUCE, emits a SPAWN intent for the grid-tactics authority to place a new unit. Owns only productionPoints (+ its spawn counter); never writes units directly.

std-round-flow.name: std-round-flow
std-round-flow.description: Headless win/lose round-resolution mechanic: derives result (victory/defeat/none) from team survivor counts on each UNITS_CHANGED signal and emits GAME_END. Owns only result; boards own their own phase/turn. Renders nothing.

std-score.name: std-score
std-score.description: Headless scoring mechanic: awards points when the enemy survivor count drops on a UNITS_CHANGED signal. Owns only score (+ its kill baseline); renders nothing.

std-survival-flow.name: std-survival-flow
std-survival-flow.description: Headless win/lose authority for the nature-survival board: owns only result. Listens RESOURCES (movesLeft) and sets result to lost when the move budget runs out before the camp is built; listens BUILD_CAMP (the render-gated win intent) and sets result to won. Renders nothing.

std-xp.name: std-xp
std-xp.description: Headless XP/progression mechanic: grants xp when the enemy survivor count drops (UNITS_CHANGED), and on reaching xpToNext levels up — raising the threshold and emitting a HEAL reward the grid-tactics authority applies. Owns only xp/xpToNext/level (+ kill baseline); never writes units.

ui-game-shell.description: GameShell — UI factory (1:1 with the @almadar/ui game-shell pattern).

std-algo-astargraph.name: std-algo-astargraph
std-algo-astargraph.description: A* engine — each tick expands the min-f (g+straight-line-h) node toward the goal on the weighted biology-canvas.

std-algo-astargrid.name: std-algo-astargrid
std-algo-astargrid.description: A* shortest path on a walled grid — each tick expands the open cell of least f=g+h, Manhattan heuristic, on cells.

std-algo-bfs.name: std-algo-bfs
std-algo-bfs.description: Breadth-first search engine — dequeues one node per tick, enqueues its unseen neighbours, recolours the graph on biology-canvas.

std-algo-binarysearch.name: std-algo-binarysearch
std-algo-binarysearch.description: Binary-search engine — one lo/hi/mid narrowing step per tick over a sorted bar array on algorithm-canvas; composed via `uses`.

std-algo-bubblesort.name: std-algo-bubblesort
std-algo-bubblesort.description: Bubble-sort engine — one adjacent compare/swap per tick over a bar array on algorithm-canvas; composed by a learning orbital via `uses`.

std-algo-dfs.name: std-algo-dfs
std-algo-dfs.description: Depth-first search engine — pops one node per tick off a LIFO stack, pushes its unseen neighbours, recolours the graph on biology-canvas.

std-algo-dijkstra.name: std-algo-dijkstra
std-algo-dijkstra.description: Dijkstra engine — each tick settles the nearest unvisited node and relaxes its weighted edges on biology-canvas.

std-algo-floodfill.name: std-algo-floodfill
std-algo-floodfill.description: Flood fill — each tick dequeues one cell, recolours it, and enqueues its 4-neighbours sharing the seed's value on algorithm-canvas cells.

std-algo-gameoflife.name: std-algo-gameoflife
std-algo-gameoflife.description: Conway's Game of Life — each tick recomputes every cell's next state from its eight neighbours, on algorithm-canvas cells.

std-algo-gridbfs.name: std-algo-gridbfs
std-algo-gridbfs.description: Grid BFS wavefront — each tick dequeues one open cell and enqueues its non-wall 4-neighbours, expanding toward the target on algorithm-canvas cells.

std-algo-heapsort.name: std-algo-heapsort
std-algo-heapsort.description: Heapsort engine — one sift-down compare/swap or extract-max micro-step per tick over a bar array on algorithm-canvas; composed by a learning orbital via `uses`.

std-algo-insertionsort.name: std-algo-insertionsort
std-algo-insertionsort.description: Insertion-sort engine — one shift/settle step per tick over a bar array on algorithm-canvas; composed by a learning orbital via `uses`.

std-algo-mazegen.name: std-algo-mazegen
std-algo-mazegen.description: Randomized-DFS maze carving — each tick carves to a random unvisited room two cells away or backtracks, on cells.

std-algo-mergesort.name: std-algo-mergesort
std-algo-mergesort.description: Bottom-up mergesort engine — one merged element copied per tick from an aux snapshot over a bar array on algorithm-canvas; composed by a learning orbital via `uses`.

std-algo-mst.name: std-algo-mst
std-algo-mst.description: Prim minimum-spanning-tree engine — each tick adds the lightest edge crossing the tree cut on biology-canvas.

std-algo-quicksort.name: std-algo-quicksort
std-algo-quicksort.description: Iterative quicksort engine — one Lomuto micro-step per tick over a bar array on algorithm-canvas, driven by an explicit range stack; composed by a learning orbital via `uses`.

std-algo-selectionsort.name: std-algo-selectionsort
std-algo-selectionsort.description: Selection-sort engine — one scan-compare or swap step per tick over a bar array on algorithm-canvas; composed by a learning orbital via `uses`.

std-algo-slidingwindow.name: std-algo-slidingwindow
std-algo-slidingwindow.description: Sliding-window engine — one fixed-size window slide per tick tracking the max-sum window on algorithm-canvas; composed via `uses`.

std-algo-toposort.name: std-algo-toposort
std-algo-toposort.description: Topological sort engine (Kahn) — each tick emits the first node whose predecessors are all ordered, recolours the DAG on biology-canvas.

std-algo-twopointer.name: std-algo-twopointer
std-algo-twopointer.description: Two-pointer engine — one left/right pair-sum step per tick over a sorted bar array on algorithm-canvas; composed via `uses`.

std-bio-cell.name: std-bio-cell
std-bio-cell.description: Eukaryotic cell-structure engine — a large steady nucleus ringed by organelles whose radii breathe at staggered phases on biology-canvas.

std-bio-foodweb.name: std-bio-foodweb
std-bio-foodweb.description: Food-web / ecosystem engine — five trophic species whose radii breathe at staggered phases (trophic lag) on biology-canvas.

std-bio-mitosis.name: std-bio-mitosis
std-bio-mitosis.description: Cell-division engine — two daughter cells separate from and rejoin a shared center each mitosis cycle via a phase-driven triangle wave on biology-canvas.

std-bio-population.name: std-bio-population
std-bio-population.description: Predator-prey population engine — Lotka-Volterra cycle via two out-of-phase oscillating node radii on biology-canvas.

std-branching-logic.name: std-branching-logic
std-branching-logic.description: std-branching-logic — survey skip-logic rules. Lists rules in `browsing`; opens rule detail in `viewing_single` with Edit / Delete actions.

std-chem-diffusion.name: std-chem-diffusion
std-chem-diffusion.description: Diffusion engine — two color-coded gases random-walk toward each other, mixing to illustrate entropy.

std-chem-osmosis.name: std-chem-osmosis
std-chem-osmosis.description: Osmosis engine — water random-walks freely while trapped solute stays right of a semipermeable membrane, so water net-crosses toward the solute to equalize concentration.

std-chem-reaction.name: std-chem-reaction
std-chem-reaction.description: Reversible reaction engine — four A+B pairs oscillate together and apart, forming and breaking a bond on chemistry-canvas.

std-math-linear.name: std-math-linear
std-math-linear.description: Linear-systems engine — line A's slope animates on a Cartesian plot; the two lines' intersection point slides with it.

std-math-numberline.name: std-math-numberline
std-math-numberline.description: Integer number-line engine — a marker hops integer-by-integer along y=0 on math-canvas, sweeping -9..9 via a phase-driven step.

std-math-sequence.name: std-math-sequence
std-math-sequence.description: Arithmetic-sequence engine — aₙ = a₀ + n·d plotted as dots on a straight line; a highlighted marker sweeps term-by-term on math-canvas.

std-math-sinewave.name: std-math-sinewave
std-math-sinewave.description: Sine wave — y = 5·sin(x − t); the whole waveform travels left as t advances (a real trig function plot).

std-math-unitcircle.name: std-math-unitcircle
std-math-unitcircle.description: Unit circle — a point sweeps the circle while its cos (horizontal) and sin (vertical) projections track on the Cartesian plot.

std-math-vectors.name: std-math-vectors
std-math-vectors.description: Vector addition engine — B oscillates via a triangle wave, animates the tip-to-tail A+B resultant on math-canvas.

std-physics-circular.name: std-physics-circular
std-physics-circular.description: Circular-motion engine — integrates bodies under a central force each tick and renders a physics-canvas via `uses`.

std-physics-collision.name: std-physics-collision
std-physics-collision.description: Elastic-collision engine — equal-mass circles exchange velocity along the collision normal and bounce off walls.

std-physics-freefall.name: std-physics-freefall
std-physics-freefall.description: Free-fall engine — bodies drop under gravity, bounce off floor/walls losing energy; composed via `uses`.

std-physics-friction.name: std-physics-friction
std-physics-friction.description: Friction engine — decelerates sliding bodies via kinetic friction each tick and renders a physics-canvas.

std-physics-gas.name: std-physics-gas
std-physics-gas.description: Ideal-gas kinetic-theory engine — many equal-mass particles bounce elastically off walls and each other in a box.

std-physics-incline.name: std-physics-incline
std-physics-incline.description: Inclined-plane engine — slides a block down a frictional ramp under gravity via line-projection every tick and renders a physics-canvas; composed by a learning orbital via `uses`, controls auto-derived from @config.

std-physics-magnetic.name: std-physics-magnetic
std-physics-magnetic.description: Magnetic cyclotron engine — integrates charged bodies under F=qv×B each tick and renders a physics-canvas via `uses`.

std-physics-orbit.name: std-physics-orbit
std-physics-orbit.description: Orbital-motion engine — integrates bodies under inverse-square gravity toward a fixed central mass every tick and renders a physics-canvas via `uses`.

std-physics-pendulum.name: std-physics-pendulum
std-physics-pendulum.description: Pendulum engine — swings a bob about a fixed pivot via rigid-length projection; renders physics-canvas via uses.

std-physics-projectile.name: std-physics-projectile
std-physics-projectile.description: Projectile-motion engine — integrates bodies under constant gravity with wall-bounce every tick and renders a physics-canvas; composed by a learning orbital via `uses`, controls auto-derived from @config.

std-physics-reflection.name: std-physics-reflection
std-physics-reflection.description: Reflection engine — photons travel straight, bouncing off box walls at equal angles; composed via `uses`.

std-physics-spring.name: std-physics-spring
std-physics-spring.description: Spring engine — Hooke's-law restoring force toward an anchor each tick; renders physics-canvas, composed via `uses`.

std-physics-wave.name: std-physics-wave
std-physics-wave.description: Wave engine — coupled oscillators; each interior point accelerates toward its neighbors' average height.

std-prob-galton.name: std-prob-galton
std-prob-galton.description: Galton board engine — balls fall under gravity and random-walk past pegs, piling into a bell curve on a physics-canvas.

std-prob-montecarlo.name: std-prob-montecarlo
std-prob-montecarlo.description: Monte-Carlo π engine — ~250 points shimmer in a [-5,5] square; the red inside-circle fraction ≈ π/4 estimates π on math-canvas.

std-prob-walk.name: std-prob-walk
std-prob-walk.description: Random-walk engine — a cloud of walkers starts at the origin and random-steps each tick into a widening Gaussian on math-canvas.

ui-learning-canvas.name: ui-learning-canvas
ui-learning-canvas.description: LearningCanvas — UI factory (1:1 with the @almadar/ui learning-canvas pattern).

ui-math-canvas.description: MathCanvas — UI factory (1:1 with the @almadar/ui math-canvas pattern).

learning-algorithms.name: learning-algorithms
learning-algorithms.description: Algorithms learning app — orbitals are individual computer-science algorithms, each composed via `uses` from a per-algorithm engine atom on algorithm-canvas (bars/cells) or biology-canvas (graphs).

learning-biology-lab.name: learning-biology-lab
learning-biology-lab.description: Biology lab organism — cells, organelles, food webs, and classification networks on a biology-canvas.

learning-biology.name: learning-biology
learning-biology.description: Biology learning app — a fat organism whose orbitals are individual biology topics, each composed via `uses` from a per-topic engine atom on the biology-canvas.

learning-chemistry-lab.name: learning-chemistry-lab
learning-chemistry-lab.description: Chemistry lab organism — molecules, reactions, atoms, bonds, and arrows on a chemistry-canvas.

learning-chemistry.name: learning-chemistry
learning-chemistry.description: Chemistry learning app — a fat organism whose orbitals are individual chemistry topics, each composed via `uses` from a per-topic engine atom on the chemistry-canvas.

learning-math-lab.name: learning-math-lab
learning-math-lab.description: Math lab organism — plots functions, vectors, geometry, number lines, slope fields, and areas on a math-canvas.

learning-math.name: learning-math
learning-math.description: Math learning app — a fat organism whose orbitals are individual math topics, each composed via `uses` from a per-topic engine atom on the math-canvas.

learning-physics-lab.name: learning-physics-lab
learning-physics-lab.description: Physics lab organism — a live gravity + wall-bounce simulation on a physics-canvas; Start/Pause/Reset drive a per-tick integrator that advances each body's velocity and position.

learning-physics.name: learning-physics
learning-physics.description: Physics learning app — a fat organism whose orbitals are individual physics topics, each composed via `uses` from a per-law engine atom. kflow presents one orbital as one lesson.

learning-probability-lab.name: learning-probability-lab
learning-probability-lab.description: Probability lab organism — dice, coins, random walks, and distributions on a math-canvas.

learning-probability.name: learning-probability
learning-probability.description: Probability learning app — a fat organism whose orbitals are individual probability topics, each composed via `uses` from a per-topic engine atom on the physics/math canvas.

std-marketing-article.name: std-marketing-article
std-marketing-article.description: Article section atom for marketing pages. Renders a centered prose block with title and rich content. Pick for about pages, mission statements, and blog-style content.

std-marketing-cta.name: std-marketing-cta
std-marketing-cta.description: Call-to-action banner atom for marketing pages. Renders a high-conversion CTA section with title, subtitle, and action buttons. Pick for conversion points at the end of pages or between sections.

std-marketing-features.name: std-marketing-features
std-marketing-features.description: Feature grid atom for marketing pages. Renders a responsive grid of feature cards with icons, titles, descriptions, and optional links. Pick for product capability showcases and benefit sections.

std-marketing-footer.name: std-marketing-footer
std-marketing-footer.description: Marketing footer atom for marketing pages. Renders a full footer with link columns, logo, and copyright. Pick for every marketing page bottom.

std-marketing-hero.name: std-marketing-hero
std-marketing-hero.description: Hero section atom for marketing pages. Renders a full-width hero with tag, title, subtitle, actions, and optional image. Pick for landing pages, product launches, and campaign microsites.

std-marketing-nav.name: std-marketing-nav
std-marketing-nav.description: Marketing navigation atom for marketing pages. Renders a sticky header with brand logo and nav links. Pick for site-wide navigation on static pages.

std-marketing-pricing.name: std-marketing-pricing
std-marketing-pricing.description: Pricing grid atom for marketing pages. Renders tiered pricing cards with plans, features, and CTAs. Pick for SaaS pricing pages and subscription showcases.

std-marketing-showcase.name: std-marketing-showcase
std-marketing-showcase.description: Showcase grid atom for marketing pages. Renders a grid of portfolio or product showcase cards with images, badges, and descriptions. Pick for product galleries and portfolio sections.

std-marketing-split.name: std-marketing-split
std-marketing-split.description: Split section atom for marketing pages. Renders a two-column layout with text and image alternating sides. Pick for feature deep-dives and alternating content sections.

std-marketing-stats.name: std-marketing-stats
std-marketing-stats.description: Stats grid atom for marketing pages. Renders a responsive grid of key metrics with values and labels. Pick for social proof, traction, and credibility sections.

std-marketing-steps.name: std-marketing-steps
std-marketing-steps.description: Step flow atom for marketing pages. Renders numbered steps horizontally or vertically. Pick for how-it-works sections and onboarding explainers.

std-marketing-team.name: std-marketing-team
std-marketing-team.description: Team grid atom for marketing pages. Renders a responsive grid of team member cards with avatars, names, roles, and bios. Pick for about pages and company culture sections.
